name: Magazine
description: An authentic print-editorial magazine template: cream paper, serif headlines and book-serif body, a single red accent, hairline rules, page folios and a masthead. Purely typographic — no photographs. Folios, kicker labels, pull quotes, Q&A blocks and crisp infographic ledgers for features, interviews and brand content.

scene_defaults.title: The Quiet Revolution
scene_defaults.narration: Inside the studios reshaping how we live and work.

fields.label: Brand / Masthead
fields.description: Publication name shown as the cover masthead brand, e.g. 'Atlas Review'

scene_defaults.title: It starts with the grid, and ends with the reader.

fields.label: Attribution
fields.description: Quote source displayed below the quote, e.g. '— Jane Smith, CEO'

fields.label: Heading
fields.description: Short editorial heading above the figures, tied to the scene's subject (e.g. 'The Numbers Behind the Launch'). Not the generic 'By the Numbers'.

fields.label: Standfirst
fields.description: Optional one-line standfirst framing the figures. Omit if there is nothing substantive to add.

fields.label: Stats
fields.description: Array of stat items, each with `value` (string, e.g. '4.2B') and `label` (string, e.g. 'Users Worldwide'), maximum 4 items

scene_defaults.title: Where does a redesign actually begin?
scene_defaults.narration: We sat down with the team behind the redesign.

exchanges.q: Where does a redesign actually begin?
exchanges.a: It begins long before anything is drawn. We spent weeks simply reading the old magazine, page by page, asking where the eye stalled and where it flew. Only once we understood how people moved through it did we touch the grid.
Type, always. A magazine lives or dies on its body text, and the temptation is to make it expressive. We resisted that — the headline can sing, but the body must disappear into the reading.

exchanges.q: How do you know when it's finished?
exchanges.a: You never quite do. But there's a moment when a spread stops feeling designed and starts feeling inevitable, as if it could not have been any other way. That is the signal to stop.

fields.label: Milestones

scene_defaults.title: A Working Theory of Print
scene_defaults.narration: The studios betting on print are not nostalgists. They have read the same charts as everyone else and reached a contrary conclusion: that attention, not paper, is the scarce resource worth fighting for. A magazine demands a kind of focus the feed cannot, and — when the columns settle and the rules align — it repays the effort. The grid becomes a quiet promise that nothing here is accidental, and that the reader's time has been respected. That promise, more than any typeface, is what keeps the format alive.

sample_props.standfirst: The studios betting on print are not nostalgists — they are chasing the scarcest resource of all.
sample_props.body: The studios betting on print are not nostalgists. They have read the same charts as everyone else and reached a contrary conclusion: that attention, not paper, is the scarce resource worth fighting for. A magazine demands a kind of focus the feed cannot, and — when the columns settle and the rules align — it repays the effort. The grid becomes a quiet promise that nothing here is accidental, and that the reader's time has been respected. That promise, more than any typeface, is what keeps the format alive.

points.value: Attention, not paper, is the scarce resource
A magazine demands a focus the feed cannot
The grid is a promise that nothing is accidental
That promise, more than type, keeps print alive

fields.label: Sectionlabel
fields.description: Red small-caps section tag displayed above the headline, e.g. 'ANALYSIS'

fields.label: Standfirst
fields.description: Single-line italic deck under the headline (≤16 words) stating the essay's sub-thesis

fields.label: Body Copy
fields.description: The full essay body that flows down the page. Leave blank to fall back to the scene narration.

fields.label: Field Notes
fields.description: Bulleted notes shown on the page; each item is one note. Leave blank to auto-split the narration into notes.

Highlight column index (0-based) for gain/loss coloring, e.g. 5 for the 6th column. Set to -1 to disable.

sample_props.leftQuote: A spread should feel inevitable — as if it could not have been set any other way.

fields.label: Quote (left ink block)
fields.description: Big statement set on the dark panel; falls back to the scene title

fields.label: Label (right block kicker)
fields.description: Small-caps label above the heading, e.g. 'Expert Profile'

fields.label: Heading (right block)
fields.description: Centred serif heading on the accent panel, e.g. a name

fields.label: Subline (right block)
fields.description: Italic line under the heading, e.g. a role or descriptor

fields.label: Tag chip (right block)
fields.description: Small inverted chip beneath the subline, e.g. 'Editor Since 2014'

scene_defaults.title: The Shape of Things to Come
scene_defaults.narration: Inside the studios redrawing how we live and work.

sample_props.body: On a wet Tuesday in a converted east-London warehouse, eleven people are arguing about a margin. It is the kind of room where the future of a magazine is decided one hairline rule at a time — where a redesign is less a grand gesture than a thousand small, stubborn judgements. The team has spent the morning on a single spread, moving the folio a few points, testing whether the drop cap should lead or follow. None of it looks like work from the outside. All of it is the work. By lunch the page reads as if it could not have been set any other way, which is exactly the point: the craft is invisible when it lands, and glaring when it does not.

fields.label: Section Label
fields.description: Red small-caps kicker above the headline, e.g. 'FEATURE'

fields.label: Article Body
fields.description: Original feature prose (60–110+ words) flowing across both pages in two columns; its first letter becomes the red drop cap. Falls back to the narration.

fields.label: Key Points
fields.description: 2–3 short takeaways (≤10 words each) shown along the bottom of both pages

scene_defaults.title: Before and After
scene_defaults.narration: The redesign, set side by side.

leftPoints.value: Columns ran too wide to read comfortably
Heavy rules cast shadows across the page
A cramped grid left the eye no room to rest

rightPoints.value: Type is set with generous air
A flexible grid guides the eye without friction

fields.label: Left Column Header
fields.description: Tracked small-caps label over the left column (accent), e.g. 'Before'

fields.label: Right Column Header
fields.description: Tracked small-caps label over the right column (ink), e.g. 'After'

fields.label: Left Column Points
fields.description: 2–6 short bullets (≤10 words each) for the left side of the comparison

fields.label: Right Column Points
fields.description: 2–6 short bullets (≤10 words each) for the right side of the comparison

fields.label: Centre Badge
fields.description: Text inside the circular centre badge; defaults to 'VS'
